title: Any podman command fails with path "/var/folders/zz/zyxvpxvq6csfxvn_n0000000000000/T/storage-run-501" exists and it is not writeable only by the current user
body:
### Issue Description

Any podman command on the CLI fails with this error message:

path "/var/folders/zz/zyxvpxvq6csfxvn_n0000000000000/T/storage-run-501" exists and it is not writeable only by the current user

Stating the poman machine from podman desktop works and shows it's running.

Even a downgrading from the latest release 5.6.1 down to 5.6.0 or 5.5.2 does not change anything (Intel Mac macOS 15.7).
I also tested a new initial install of 5.6.1 on another Intel Mac on macOS 15.7 with the same result.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Fresh install in an Intel Mac with macOS 15.7 (released yesterady)
2. Run podman on the CLI, like _podman info_
3. Exact same error message: path "/var/folders/zz/zyxvpxvq6csfxvn_n0000000000000/T/storage-run-501" exists and it is not writeable only by the current user


### Describe the results you received

path "/var/folders/zz/zyxvpxvq6csfxvn_n0000000000000/T/storage-run-501" exists and it is not writeable only by the current user

### Describe the results you expected

Normal operations of podman CLI commands.

### podman info output

```yaml
If you are unable to run podman info for any reason, please provide the podman version, operating system and its version and the architecture you are running.

Podman 5.6.1 (also tested 5.6.0 and 5.5.2)
macOS 15.7 (24G222)
Intel/amd64
```

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Using direct distribution install from podman.io/Github on host OS.

### Additional information

I discoverd this after a reboot due to an macOS update to macOS 15.7.
This issue might be realted to that update, but I did updates to podman desktop and podman (via podman desktop, both installed via their official distrubutions) recently too.
On one machine there where also several updates via homebrew, but on the 2nd Mac homebrew is not in use at all.

There might be a relation to this issue https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/27107 too, but I see this issue with any podman command, i.e. when trying to run an existing container image, that worked before.

comments.body: I´ve had two occurences of this error in MacOS (arm) when running podman from terminal within the Zed editor. When the issue occurs I get the same error when running terminal by itself. Downgrade in brew also didn´t resolve the issue. I did a full shutdown of embedded terminal and application which resolved it for me (on first occurance I did a MacOS reboot).
+1 - had to fully terminate the Terminal application, then it worked.
@DanielFroehlich thanks that worked for some reason. Which terminal are you using? Warp?
Same here after reboot withot any update. Terminal.app was started automatically after reboot. Quit and starting Terminmal.app fixed the issue.
just to be sure, if you run it from the CLI only (no podman desktop to start and stop), do you still observe this problem.
@baude yes, I experienced the issue in both standalone and editor embedded terminal simultaneously.

@baude Need to correct myself. having the issue now and the issue is only present in embedded terminal in Zed. Works if I open a new standalone terminal and start podman, or a fresh terminal inside Zed.

**Steps to replicate the issue:**
1. open zsh terminal
2. use podman to start machine, then start a container
3. use podman to stop container, then stop machine
4. without closing terminal, log out user
5. after user login the error should be observable in the terminal

I can confirm this fix works as of 10/23/2025
Apple M2
15.7.1 (24G231)
terminal: zsh
zsh 5.9 (arm64-apple-darwin24.0)

This is definitely a mac issue and not a podman issue. Similar report in unrelated project: https://github.com/google-gemini/gemini-cli/issues/8690

Ran into this today after upgrading to macos 15.7.1. Had to cmd+Q my terminal app (Ghostty) and on a fresh launch it stopped happening.
I'm going to close this one given it seems like not a Podman issue
